codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly exposes resolved market behavior in the user-facing UI with explicit outcomes for majority, tie, and no-vote cases. All expected behaviors are covered: markets stay unresolved before close, resolved markets show explicit outcomes, voting is disabled on resolved markets, and state markers are consistent across list and detail views. The test coverage is comprehensive with 21 tests covering all VAL-RESOLVE assertions and VAL-CROSS-006.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Both market_detail_live.ex and market_list_live.ex define the same helper functions: `format_status/1` and `is_winner?/2`. This duplication was flagged in the prior voting milestone scrutiny review as a non-blocking issue. The worker continued this pattern rather than extracting shared logic.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib/predictions_web/live/market_detail_live.ex:166-170 and lib/predictions_web/live/market_list_live.ex:111-115 contain identical function definitions for format_status/1 and is_winner?/2. The misc-voting-code-hygiene-followups feature already exists to address this.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the phoenix-fullstack-worker skill procedure correctly: wrote failing tests first, invoked elixir-expert skill, implemented the smallest end-to-end slice, ran focused tests during iteration, performed HTTP verification, and ran mix precommit. The handoff confirms followedProcedure: true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows: Read mission/validation artifacts, invoked elixir-expert and phoenix-fullstack-worker skills, created test file first, saw failing tests, then implemented, ran tests, performed HTTP verification, and ran mix precommit. handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true.

summary: PASS - The resolved market state presentation feature is well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage (21 tests) covering all required behaviors. The implementation correctly shows explicit outcomes for majority (winner badge and label), tie (multiple tied options message), and no-vote scenarios. Voting is properly disabled on resolved markets. State markers are consistent between list and detail views. Code quality is good with proper use of Phoenix LiveView patterns, data attributes for testing, and Tailwind styling. The only observation is continuation of the known code-duplication pattern for format_status/1, which is already tracked in the misc-voting-code-hygiene-followups feature.
